ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-08-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following two cases were called together: PTR-04-286133 The Fickert Revocable Living Trust and PCN-04-286847 Conservatorship of Edward Charles Fickert.

Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee). Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Conservator).

Mr. Der addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the Trustee's fees, title of the property, the reverse mortgage loan and how the tax penality was incurred. He further stated that he will file Ms. Dolch's time record and that she is resigning in favor of Thomas Lucas and that they want to have their fees paid now. Mr. Siracusa stated that the Conservatee may outlive the funds and that the Conservator's fees will be a lien on the property. Both matters were taken under submission for the Court to review the time records.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Sr. Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-07-2013 Continued To Jan-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-26-2012 Continued To Jan-07-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-15-2012 Continued To Nov-26-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-04-286847 Conservatorship of Edward Charles Fickert.

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Conservator). Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Trustee).

The Court expressed concern about the request for fees, whether there was duplication of fees and whether it is appropriate for the Conservator to end up getting fees in excess of the value of the conservatorship assets. Mr. Der stated that his Client is willing to resign as Trustee and have Mr. Lucas step up as Successor Trustee, and suggested continuing the matters so that he amend his petition to be a final account and petition for appointment of Successor Trustee. Mr. Siracusa spoke of the work performed by his Client, stated that his Client did not bill for some of his work, addressed the grocery bills and the cable bill. Mr. Der will include time sheets as part of the amendment. Both accounting petitions were continued to November 26, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-04-286847 Conservatorship of Edward Charles Fickert.

Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Trustee). Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Conservator).

Mr. Siracusa stated that they needed additional funds to pay for the Conservatee's care, that they worked with the Trustee on a reverse mortgage, that the lender required that the Trustee and the Conservator sign the documents, that they discussed a mechanism for paying the bills and that they were fine with either proposal. Mr. Der stated that he was happy to do whatever the Court likes and that it was most efficient for Mr. Lucas to handle the bill paying. Mr. Siracusa also stated that bond in the amount of $454,000.00 was needed to cover the credit line. Status of filing of bond in the Conservatorship matter was set for October 18, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the petitions (subject to review of the orders).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
